across: 2. Particular point or place in physical space.
5. Opening in anything made by breaking or parting.
10. Part of Earth which is not covered by oceans or other bodies of water.
11. Large extent or tract of land; for example a region, country or district.

hints.across-2: Determination of the location of something
hints.across-3: The basin-like opening or mouth of a volcano, through which the chief eruption comes; similarly, the mouth of a geyser, about which a cone of silica is often built up.
hints.down-4: The section of a passenger plane having the same class of service.
hints.down-7: Engine operating similarly to the pyrotechnic, generating thrust by the expulsion of hot gases; a rocket engine.
hints.down-8: The largest dwarf planet and formerly the ninth planet, represented by the symbol ♇ or ⯓, both now used mostly in astrology.
hints.down-9: Central part of a fruit, containing the kernels or seeds.
hints.across-10: Real estate or landed property; a partitioned and measurable area which is owned and acquired and on which buildings and structures can be built and erected.
hints.across-11: One of three of Canada's federal entities, located in the country's Arctic, with fewer powers than a province and created by an act of Parliament rather than by the Constitution: Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut.
